meta.name: Agent Design Framework
meta.topic: AI agents managing work for humans
meta.inspiration: Apple WWDC / Tesla AI Day keynotes

palette.description: Agent-specific palette: deep space dark anchors trust, cyan-teal signals autonomous intelligence, warm amber marks human-in-the-loop moments

accent.description: Teal-cyan — signals machine intelligence, autonomy, agent 'alive' state
accent.description2: Electric violet — represents neural networks, reasoning, cognition

usage.primary60: bg.dark — used on 60-70% of slides as dominant background
usage.secondary30: bg.darkCard / bg.light — panels, cards, sandwich slides
usage.accent10: accent.primary / accent.secondary — headlines, icons, data viz, CTAs

hero.usage: Title slide only — one per deck

slideTitleOnLight.usage: Content slide titles on light backgrounds

bodyLarge.usage: Key messages, comparison headers, callout text

caption.usage: Labels, footnotes, axis labels, source citations

spacing.gutterNote: 100-120px equivalent — breathing room from slide edges
spacing.elementPadNote: Internal padding within cards and shape containers
spacing.sectionGapNote: Vertical space between major content blocks on a slide

motif.name: icons-in-teal-circles
motif.description: Every slide gets at least one react-icons/fa icon rendered inside a colored circle
motif.placement: Top-left of content area or inline with section headers

shadows.note: CRITICAL: NEVER reuse shadow objects in pptxgenjs — always call factory functions to get fresh instances

cardShadow.implementation: () => ({ type: 'outer', blur: 8, offset: 3, angle: 270, color: '000000', opacity: 0.25 })

darkCardShadow.implementation: () => ({ type: 'outer', blur: 12, offset: 4, angle: 270, color: '000000', opacity: 0.5 })

accentGlow.implementation: () => ({ type: 'outer', blur: 20, offset: 0, angle: 0, color: '00E5C8', opacity: 0.15 })

transitions.rule: Apply to EVERY slide — no exceptions

decorativeShape.description: Subtle gradient arc in bottom-right corner — signals depth and futurism

DARK_BG.usage: Default for 60-70% of slides — main content, comparisons, diagrams

cardStyle.shadow: USE createCardShadow() — fresh instance per card

LIGHT_BG.usage: Sandwich slides — break visual monotony every 3-4 dark slides

decorativeShape.description: Full-width thin accent line above the section title

SECTION.usage: Section dividers — centered titles, minimal content, pause-and-breathe moments

rules: NEVER place accent-colored lines or bars directly under slide titles
NEVER reuse pptxgenjs option objects — always create fresh objects or call factory functions
NEVER use # prefix in hex color strings — pptxgenjs expects raw hex like 00E5C8 not #00E5C8
Do NOT center body text — always left-align; center alignment is ONLY for titles on SECTION slides
Every slide MUST have at least one visual element: icon-in-circle motif, shape, chart, or image
Use maximum 2 font families across the entire deck: Georgia for headers, Calibri for body
Use maximum 4 font sizes on any single slide — pick from the typography scale
NEVER repeat the same layout on consecutive slides — vary between full-width, 2-col, 3-col, card grid, chart
Use { bullet: true } for bullet lists — never use unicode bullet characters like • or ▸
Use { breakLine: true } when passing multi-line text as arrays to pptxgenjs
Set { margin: 0 } on text options when precise alignment with adjacent shapes is needed
Do NOT use ROUNDED_RECTANGLE shape with accent-colored borders — it renders inconsistently
Include at least 1 chart slide (bar, doughnut, or line) for data-driven content
Apply transitions { type: 'fade', speed: 0.6 } to every single slide — no exceptions
Maintain the 60-30-10 color ratio: 60% dark bg, 30% secondary surfaces, 10% accent pops
Icon motif circles use low-opacity fill (15%) with a thin accent border — not solid fill
Cards on dark backgrounds use darkCard fill (141929) — never transparent or pure black
Cards on light backgrounds use white fill (FFFFFF) with outer shadow from createCardShadow()
Keep gutter spacing at 1.1 inches from all slide edges for consistent breathing room
Section divider slides should have NO body text — only section title and optional subtitle

layoutVariations.description: Rotate through these layouts — never use the same one twice in a row

options.name: chart-with-callout
options.usage: Data slides — chart on left, key takeaway on right
